prompt: A Local Peer Exchange & Ledger Management system with main features including peer matching, identity and credit governance, role-based access control, transparent billing traceability, reputation scoring, and operations analytics. Regular Users create a local account, complete a real-name status workflow, and then request a peer session or exchange by selecting tags (topic/category), preferred time slots, and availability; the interface (built with HTMX for fast partial-page updates) shows a live queue panel with clear states such as “Searching,” “Match Found,” or “Retrying,” and allows either manual initiation or auto-matching. Auto-matching runs every 10 seconds while the request is active, times out after 3 minutes, and retries up to 3 times with a 30-second cool-down; users may set a temporary “do not match” list, and the system enforces frequency controls such as a maximum of 10 match attempts per hour and a 2-minute minimum interval between cancellations. After a completed session, both parties are prompted to submit a 1–5 star rating plus an optional comment; reputations display as a composite score that factors close rate, cancellation rate, and dispute rate, while sensitive details (ID numbers, documents) are always masked on-screen. Administrators review violation flags, appeals, and bans, manage resource permissions down to building/classroom/time-slot equivalents for the organization’s schedule inventory, and use KPI dashboards to track conversion, average order value, repurchase, and dispute rates with date filters, CSV export, and offline scheduled report generation at 2:00 AM local time. The backend uses Flask to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX, validating all critical actions server-side with idempotency keys for retried submissions and strict risk checks (for example, blocking match requests when a user is muted/banned, when credit score is below 60, or when there are more than 3 open disputes). SQLite persists users, real-name status events, encrypted ID document blobs, matching queue entries, blacklists, ratings, violations, appeals, and ledgers. ID documents are stored locally using AES-256 encryption with a server-held key, and only file type/size validation and SHA-256 fingerprinting are performed (no OCR or cloud scanning); access is restricted to Administrators with audited viewing. The ledger module maintains tamper-evident traceability by chaining audit records with hashes across invoice/voucher events, supporting AR/AP calculation, overdue rules (net 15 by default, marked overdue at 12:00 AM the day after), and reconciliation summaries; all edits after posting require an adjusting entry rather than mutation. Authentication is strictly offline with username and password (minimum 12 characters, lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts), and authorization combines roles (Regular User, Administrator, Auditor) with fine-grained resource permissions and row-level data isolation, producing immutable audit logs for login, export/import, permission changes, and key financial actions. Optional payments and order flows, when enabled, are limited to offline recorded payments (cash/check/ACH reference entry) with local “callback” simulation for testing, signature verification using locally stored keys, and refund handling via reversing entries so the system remains fully functional without any external network dependencies.
